Through our work at KevinRoot Medical, we support thousands of physicians across the country. Given today’s legal and regulatory environment, I strongly recommend that all physicians take proactive steps to protect themselves and their families. The asset-protection structures established by LegalMine provide a level of security and confidence that I believe is essential—whether facing malpractice risk, automobile liability, or other unforeseen events. I would encourage every physician in our network to seriously consider putting these protections in place.

I strongly recommend that physicians protect themselves and their families using the legal structures established by LegalMine. I have far greater confidence knowing I’m protected in the event of car accidents, malpractice claims, or other unforeseen liabilities. The United States offers tremendous freedom—but that freedom also requires personal responsibility. LegalMine helped me put the right protections in place.

Legally Mine has been a disappointment. I signed up for their service after hearing from Gary at a medical conference. The pitch was to medical professionals wanting to protect themselves from lawsuits.
The communication from their team has been terrible. I consulted with Dustin for help with filing an LLC and he lied and told me it was being taken care of. After multiple emails, I found out that nothing had been done and the start of my business was delayed by months after my misplaced trust that they were doing what they said they would do.
We filled out paperwork for the creation of LLCs and have been left in the dark regarding what is going on. I've been told they are short-staffed, that our file won't be done for months, etc, etc. I reached out to Gary since his pitch was the reason we signed with this company and my emails have been ignored.
They haven't bothered to explain the process, the timeline, or communicate any expectations.
There has been a total lack of customer service and support, which creates unneeded frustration and distrust.
I would recommend looking elsewhere. This has been a waste of time and money. We could have created our own LLCs for all of the guidance we've received from this company.

We've used Legally Mine for many years for asset protection and estate planning for personal and business needs.  Being in  business since the late 1980's and can tell you that these guys are probably in my top 5 (maybe higher) ranked by most responsive, satisfied with their work, professional, get things done in a very quick timeframe...............the list goes on.  Legally Mine has set up 20+ entities for us and created a very simple yet comprehensive "Blueprint" of our assets.  Lenders are quite impressed with the document showing this, and how they are all connected.  In addition to the asset protection side, they have also put together a very detailed estate plan showing where all assets are to go after we're out of the picture.

It's rare to get a company that has it all and be100% satisfied with them from day one.  Legally Mine has been that for our family and businesses.  I would highly recommend them for these services.  You won't be disappointed.

Terry Boone
TEAM MANAGEMENT PROPERTIES, LLC / ARKAD Manufacturing, Inc. / The Clean Machine Laundry

reviews.text: Dr. Gurney is probably the most skilled Chiropractor I have visited in 30 years! However, he is a bit expensive, his staff isn't very friendly, and when I had a billing issue they weren't helpful in helping me resolve it. I don't think I will be going back.

Aspen Chiropractic is an excellent place for treatment. I have seen quite a few chiropractors, and I think Dr. Gurney is definitely one of the better ones in terms of knowledge and understanding of the human body/alignment. I’ve had chiropractors make adjustments prior to properly aligning my body. Dr. Gurney is very good at finding the places that require adjustment. He uses a unique method, which they call muscle testing, in order to find the areas of the body that require realignment. This is a technique that I’d never experienced before, but after experiencing it, I can find it very valid. It is my understanding that this muscle testing isn’t generally covered by insurance (my insurance doesn’t cover it)… I saw that the reviewer below didn’t want to pay the $12 for the muscle testing… yeah, money doesn’t grow on trees, but the muscle testing is worth 12 bucks if you are going to a chiropractor. I had a pulled hamstring that was highly problematic, and I had no idea that I was pulling it more and more, worsening the condition (I’m a yogi), until I’d had the muscle testing. It’s solved a lot of problems. I also appreciate that Dr. Gurney is very experienced with extremities. I have relatively flat feet, and my feet would not hold in alignment because of the poor condition of the muscles. In a matter of under 10 visits, my flat feet are now holding the correct form. My pulled hamstring is almost all healed (there is some inflammation at the points that attach to the bone… to be expected). My back feels great, and I’m in the mountains backpacking/hiking/climbing almost every weekend, so I’m not taking it easy at all. Dr. Gurney is great. I was very disheartened to see my other reviewer did not have as good of an experience, but please don’t let that thwart you… he’s a very good doctor. I can see where he can come off as a little flustered (per the other reviewer), but I think this is more a reflection of having so much information to offer, and not a lack of preparation. There have been a few times I needed to remind him to check my feet, but all it took was the reminder, and then he took care of it. If you are like me, and pretty much “captain active,” he’s quite excellent with that. I’ve also seen him very effectively heal injury-related mis-alignments (through friends’ experiences, not my own). Overall, I’d highly recommend Dr. Gurney.

Dr. Gurney was a joke. I was recommended to him, so thought I would be in capable hands, but his care was unacceptable. He was flustered while asking questions about where my pain was and then didn't take x-rays to check my spine to make informed decisions about treatment. He kept suggesting additional treatments that "insurance doesn't cover" and some of it was seriously ridiculous. He wanted to use a "muscular strength test" to determine what adjustments to make because regular adjustments "aren't as exact." I asked him to tell me what it involved before I paid for it and he said "It's hard to explain, I have to show you to really explain it, but it helps me be more exact and know where to move the bones." The test involved him pushing muscles before making adjustments - so I told him I didn't see how it was different than a standard adjustment should be, and said I wasn't interested. He continued to explain it more, I continued to say I don't think it does anything, so I'd rather he didn't.

My neck was really tight when he tried to adjust it, so when it didn't crack on the first try, he proceeded to force it THREE MORE TIMES. It was very painful and never adjusted. After the sub-par "adjustments" of my neck and upper back, he tried to set me up for another appointment; so I asked if he was going to check the rest of back and joints and do a full examination. He was surprised and said, "Oh yes, let's go ahead do that now."

When I left, the receptionist charged me for the muscle test I told him I didn't want, and twice for the exam and adjustment, saying they aren't the same....I told her I didn't want the muscle test and she said, next time I can make a note for him to not include it, but he did perform it this time. He is clearly incompetent and trying to squeeze every penny out of patients. I would not recommend him to anyone.

I understand the vehicle was a good price but what’s another at most $1000 to replace rear brake rotors and pads and put on some new tires, heck even just a decent basic cheapie tire so your not sending someone home two hours in winter on worn out flat summer tires. reviews.text:
Our experience buying a car was so so. We found a car we liked but didn’t like how there was some dents. The salesman was quick to reach out and pushy to get us to come back in, drive it again, and go over the numbers. When we did, the numbers didn’t match and we had to talk and go back and forth to get down to what the car was even listed on the website for. They said they were going to fix the car as well. I was pre approved for a loan yet they still ran a credit application without my permission and then made me sign the consent after it was already pulled. Very annoyed on that part. We then had to wait multiple hours for the signing office to be available to actually sign.

Then when we were in the signing office, we asked to make sure that the dent would be fixed but the salesman never got that written up… if we hadn’t spoken up then I’m sure they would not have honored it. Luckily they did get that added as a “we owe” document.

Getting the dent fix was another headache. We bright back the car, were told that it would take 4-5 days and heard nothing else. When I reached out asking if it was done, we were told that it wasn’t fixable and that it would be another 2 weeks. No communication at all before this as it was all initiated by me. You would think that is there’s a big difference in plan that the salesman would reach out to at least give you an update. We decided to get back the car while we waited for a whole new door to get shipped in. Once it was, we brought back the car yet again and given a timeframe. I once again reached out asking about the status and was told it would take longer than they thought.

Very disappointed in running my credit when they didn’t need to or ask and lack of communication. Of course they are great about responding when it could be a deal for them.

All in all we are happy with the Toyota we got from them but don’t expect great communication or a quick experience. And be cautious about credit pulls.

reviews.text:
I brought my 2022 VW Taos to Ken Garff for a routine oil change. About two weeks later, the check engine light came on. After scanning it, the vehicle showed a P0491 (secondary air injection system – insufficient flow) code — an issue in an area that is accessed when the engine cover is removed during service.

When I contacted the dealership to ask them to take a look since the issue appeared shortly after their service, they immediately refused responsibility and told me I would need to pay $198 just for a diagnosis, despite the fact that the problem appeared
right after my visit. They dismissed any possibility that something could have been disturbed during the oil change and hid behind the vehicle being out of warranty.

I wasn’t asking for free repairs — I was asking for accountability and a basic inspection after a service that may have caused the issue. Instead, I was met with deflection and zero willingness to help.

Very disappointing experience. An oil change should not result in a check engine light and a potential expensive repair, especially when the dealership refuses to even look at it without charging. This feels like poor customer service and a lack of integrity. I won’t be returning.
